title: Botulinum Toxins A and B — UHC Medical Policy (Chronic Migraine Prophylaxis)
sourceText: Extracted from UHC medical policy document: botulinum-toxins-a-and-b-cs.pdf. Criteria reflect actual UHC coverage requirements for onabotulinumtoxinA (J0585) for chronic migraine prophylaxis.

criteria.text: Diagnosis of chronic migraine defined by ALL of: ≥15 headache days/month, ≥8 migraine days/month, headaches last ≥4 hours/day
criteria.evidenceHint: Look for explicit headache frequency (days/month), migraine-quality day count, and headache duration documentation in neurology notes or headache diary entries.

criteria.text: History of failure (after a trial of at least two months), contraindication, or intolerance to prophylactic therapy with one agent from two of the following classes: Antidepressant (amitriptyline, venlafaxine), Antiepileptic (divalproex, topiramate), Beta blocker (atenolol, propranolol, nadolol, timolol, metoprolol)
criteria.evidenceHint: Look for documented trials of at least two agents from two distinct drug classes listed in the policy, each with a trial duration of at least two months OR documented contraindication/intolerance. Note the policy requires only TWO classes — verify which classes are represented by each documented agent.
criteria.uploadHint: Upload a physician addendum or prior treatment summary documenting: (1) drug name and class for each trial, (2) start and end dates confirming ≥2 months per trial, and (3) reason for discontinuation (failure, intolerance, or contraindication). A pharmacy medication history printout also satisfies this requirement.

criteria.text: Botox dose does not exceed 155 units administered intramuscularly divided over 31 injection sites across 7 head and neck muscles every 12 weeks
criteria.evidenceHint: Look for an explicit dosing statement in the plan or order section specifying total units, number of injection sites, and administration interval. Must match 155 units / 31 sites / every 12 weeks.
